concerns.text: Task T012a mandates validating the full raw pipeline on CI using 'synthetic NIfTI data and mock tool invocations'. This violates FR-007 which requires the system to 'execute the entire analysis pipeline... on a GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest runner' and Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) which demands results be reproducible by re-running code against data. Mocks/synthetic data do not constitute a valid re-run against real data, effectively weakening the FR-007 constraint to a 'logic check' rather than an 'execution' requirement.

Task T026 implements '% Confidence Interval calculation' and explicitly states it 'replaces the Spec's FR-008 post-hoc power analysis'. FR-008 explicitly requires a 'post-hoc power analysis to calculate the detectable effect size (r)'. Replacing this deliverable with a different statistical metric (CI) without a spec amendment constitutes a silent relaxation of the functional requirement and violates SC-005 which requires the report to include a 'power analysis'.

Tasks T023a/T023b introduce a PCA/MANOVA approach to derive a single 'Network Architecture' factor. While the plan mentions this, FR-005 and SC-003 strictly require multiple-comparison correction (FDR) to be applied to the *individual* network metrics (modularity, global efficiency, etc.). The task list lacks an explicit step to perform FDR on the raw individual metrics, risking a deliverable that only reports the PCA factor, thereby failing the specific FDR requirement for individual metrics mandated by the spec.

Task T027 implements logging of a correlation threshold (r > 0.3). Constitution Principle VII requires the analysis script to log the threshold 'after applying the specified multiple-comparison correction'. The task description does not explicitly link this logging step to the post-FDR results, creating a potential compliance gap where the logged threshold might be applied to raw p-values, violating the provenance requirements of the Constitution.
